info.name: 🚖 Vehicle Bookings API
info.description:
# Vehicle Bookings API

A scalable and production-ready vehicle booking platform built with **Node.js**, **Express.js v5**, **MongoDB Atlas**, and **Mongoose v9**.

## Base URL
```
http://localhost:5000/api/v1
```

## Features
- ✅ Complete CRUD Operations
- ✅ Pagination with Configurable Limits
- ✅ Soft Delete & Hard Delete
- ✅ Data Validation & Sanitization
- ✅ MVC Architecture

## Dataset
- **18,289 real ride-hailing records** from Bangalore, India
- **7 vehicle types**: Prime Sedan, Prime SUV, Prime Plus, Mini, Auto, Bike, eBike
- **50 unique locations** across Bangalore

## Response Format
All APIs return a standard JSON response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Description of the result",
  "data": { ... }
}
```

## Error Codes
| Code | Meaning |
|------|----------|
| 200 | Success |
| 201 | Created |
| 400 | Bad Request (validation error) |
| 404 | Not Found |
| 409 | Conflict (already deleted) |
| 500 | Internal Server Error |

---

**Developer:** Vedant Patel

**GitHub:** [vedantxy/vehicle_bookings_vedant_patel](https://github.com/vedantxy/vehicle_bookings_vedant_patel)

item.name: 📝 Create Booking

request.description:
## Create a New Vehicle Booking

Creates a new booking record in the database.

### Required Fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `customerName` | String | Full name of the customer |
| `vehicleType` | String | One of: `sedan`, `suv`, `hatchback`, `luxury` |
| `pickupLocation` | String | Pickup address |
| `dropLocation` | String | Drop-off address |
| `distance` | Number | Distance in km (must be ≥ 0) |
| `fare` | Number | Fare amount in ₹ (must be ≥ 0) |
| `paymentMethod` | String | One of: `cash`, `card`, `upi`, `net_banking` |

### Optional Fields
| Field | Type | Default |
|-------|------|---------|
| `customerPhone` | String | `null` |
| `bookingStatus` | String | `pending` |
| `paymentStatus` | String | `pending` |
| `rating` | Number | `null` (1.0 - 5.0) |
| `bookingDate` | Date | Current date |
| `rideStartTime` | Date | `null` |
| `rideEndTime` | Date | `null` |

### Validation Rules
- `distance` and `fare` must be non-negative numbers
- `rating` must be between 1 and 5 (if provided)
- All string inputs are automatically trimmed and sanitized
- Enum fields are case-insensitive (e.g., `"SUV"` → `"suv"`)

### Response: `201 Created`

response.name: Success - Booking Created
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Booking created successfully",
  "data": {
    "_id": "665c1a2b3f4e5d6a7b8c9d0e",

    "customerPhone": "+919876543210",
    "vehicleType": "suv",
    "pickupLocation": "Koramangala, Bangalore",
    "dropLocation": "Indiranagar, Bangalore",
    "distance": 12.5,
    "fare": 350,
    "bookingStatus": "pending",
    "paymentMethod": "upi",
    "paymentStatus": "pending",
    "rating": 4.5,
    "bookingDate": "2026-06-01T10:30:00.000Z",
    "rideStartTime": "2026-06-01T10:45:00.000Z",
    "rideEndTime": "2026-06-01T11:15:00.000Z",
    "isDeleted": false,

    "updatedAt": "2026-06-01T10:30:00.000Z",
    "__v": 0
  }
}

response.name: Error - Invalid Vehicle Type

request.description:
## Create Booking with Minimum Required Fields

Demonstrates creating a booking with only the required fields. All optional fields will use their default values:
- `bookingStatus` → `"pending"`
- `paymentStatus` → `"pending"`
- `bookingDate` → Current date/time
- `rating` → `null`

## Data Sanitization Test

This request demonstrates how the API handles dirty/messy data:

| Input | Dirty Value | Cleaned Value |
|-------|-------------|---------------|
| `customerName` | `"  Priya Sharma  "` | `"Priya Sharma"` (trimmed) |
| `vehicleType` | `"  SUV  "` | `"suv"` (trimmed + lowercased) |
| `distance` | `"18.5 km"` | `18.5` (parsed to number) |
| `fare` | `"275"` | `275` (string → number) |
| `bookingStatus` | `"PENDING"` | `"pending"` (lowercased) |
| `rating` | `"4.2"` | `4.2` (string → number) |

The `dataCleaner.js` utility automatically handles all these transformations.

item.description:
## POST /api/v1/bookings

Endpoints for creating new vehicle bookings. Includes examples with full fields, minimum fields, and dirty data sanitization testing.

item.name: 📖 Read Bookings

request.description:
## Get All Bookings

Retrieves all active (non-deleted) bookings with **default pagination** (page 1, limit 10).

### Default Behavior
- Returns first 10 records
- Excludes soft-deleted bookings (`isDeleted: false`)
- Includes pagination metadata

### Response includes:
```json
{
  "pagination": {
    "total": 18289,
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 10,
    "totalPages": 1829
  }
}
```

## Get Bookings with Custom Pagination

Retrieves page 2 with 20 records per page.

### Pagination Parameters
| Parameter | Type | Default | Max | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-----|-------------|
| `page` | Number | 1 | - | Page number |
| `limit` | Number | 10 | 100 | Records per page |

### How Pagination Works
- `skip = (page - 1) × limit`
- For page 2, limit 20: skip = 20, returns records 21-40
- Invalid values auto-correct: `page=-1` → `1`, `limit=500` → `100`

### Performance
Uses `Promise.all()` to run `countDocuments` and `find` queries in parallel for optimal database performance.

item.name: Get All Bookings (Large Page - Safety Cap Test)

request.description:
## Safety Cap Test

Tests the pagination safety cap. Even though `limit=500` is requested, the API caps it at **100** to protect the database from massive scans.

Expected: `pagination.limit` will be `100`, not `500`.

## Get a Single Booking by MongoDB ObjectId

Retrieves a specific booking document by its `_id`.

### Path Parameter
| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `id` | String | MongoDB ObjectId (24-character hex string) |

### Validations
1. **ObjectId format check** — Returns `400` if ID format is invalid
2. **Soft delete awareness** — Returns `404` if booking is soft-deleted
3. **Existence check** — Returns `404` if booking doesn't exist

### Usage
Replace `{{bookingId}}` with an actual MongoDB ObjectId from the GET All response.

**Example:** `GET /api/v1/bookings/665c1a2b3f4e5d6a7b8c9d0e`

item.description:
## GET /api/v1/bookings

Endpoints for retrieving bookings. Supports pagination and single-record lookup by ID.

item.name: ✏️ Update Booking

request.description:
## Update a Booking (Full Update)

Updates one or more fields of an existing booking.

### Security: Whitelist Approach
Only these 14 fields can be updated:
`customerName`, `customerPhone`, `vehicleType`, `pickupLocation`, `dropLocation`, `distance`, `fare`, `bookingStatus`, `paymentMethod`, `paymentStatus`, `rating`, `bookingDate`, `rideStartTime`, `rideEndTime`

⚠️ Fields like `isDeleted`, `_id`, `createdAt` are **ignored** even if sent — this prevents mass assignment attacks.

### Field-Specific Sanitization
- **Strings** → Trimmed
- **Numbers** → Parsed from string if needed
- **Enums** → Lowercased automatically
- **Dates** → Parsed to ISO format

### Validations
- `distance` ≥ 0
- `fare` ≥ 0
- `rating` between 1-5
- Mongoose schema validators run via `runValidators: true`

## Update Booking Status Only (Partial Update)

Updates **only** the `bookingStatus` field — faster and safer than a full PUT.

### Allowed Status Values
| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `pending` | Booking created, awaiting confirmation |
| `confirmed` | Driver assigned, ride confirmed |
| `completed` | Ride finished successfully |
| `cancelled` | Booking cancelled |

### Validation
- Status value is **case-insensitive** (`"COMPLETED"` → `"completed"`)
- Invalid status returns `400 Bad Request`
- Soft-deleted bookings return `404 Not Found`

item.description:
## PUT & PATCH /api/v1/bookings/:id

Endpoints for updating booking records. Supports full update (PUT) and partial status update (PATCH).

item.name: 🗑️ Delete Booking

request.description:
## Soft Delete a Booking (Recommended)

Marks a booking as deleted by setting `isDeleted = true` **without removing it from the database**.

### Why Soft Delete?
In production systems, data should never be permanently deleted because:
- Compliance & audit requirements
- Data recovery needs
- Analytics & historical reporting

### Behavior
- Sets `isDeleted: true` on the booking document
- All GET APIs filter by `isDeleted: false`, so soft-deleted bookings become invisible
- Returns `409 Conflict` if booking is already soft-deleted (prevents double deletion)

### After Soft Delete
- `GET /bookings` → Will NOT include this booking
- `GET /bookings/:id` → Will return `404 Not Found`
- `PATCH /bookings/:id/soft-delete` again → Will return `409 Conflict`

## Hard Delete a Booking (Permanent)

⚠️ **DANGER**: Permanently removes the booking from the database. This action is **irreversible**.

### When to Use
- Only for test data cleanup
- Never in production without admin authorization

### Behavior
- Uses `findByIdAndDelete()` to permanently remove the document
- Returns `404` if booking doesn't exist
- No recovery possible after hard delete

item.description:
## DELETE & PATCH /api/v1/bookings/:id

Endpoints for deleting bookings. Supports both **soft delete** (recommended, reversible) and **hard delete** (permanent, irreversible).
